marketState: neutral, tilted bullish near-term on extreme summer heat demand, capped medium-term by storage surplus and weak/unreliable seasonality
summary: Front price ($2.948) is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.031, FAIR), so there is no fair-value edge today, though the fair-value path itself rises to $3.067 by +21D. The dominant near-term driver is weather: cooling demand is running 15.4% above the 5-year norm over the next week and 23.9% above over days 8-16, both near the top of their historical ranges (rangePos 0.90-0.93) and still climbing (+24.5 in the last 24h, +20% vs the 5y normal trend). That supports a short-term bounce, reinforced by daily/4h technicals that are oversold (RSI 26-36, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) just as intraday momentum (5m/15m RSI 59-66, MACD positive) is already turning up, and by front-month backwardation versus Sep (-$0.048) consistent with near-term tightness. Working against a longer commitment: seasonality shows only 31-38% odds of a higher price over 1-4 weeks with a -3.1% typical 4-week move, but that read is explicitly flagged Low reliability; storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average after a build that beat forecast by 72 Bcf (bearish surprise); and daily/weekly technicals carry a 50<200 EMA cross. None of these are extreme (managed money is only net short at the 35th 5y percentile), so the medium-term picture is a genuine standoff between high-priority bullish weather and lower-priority bearish fundamentals/technicals rather than a clean directional edge.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (top priority) is bearish-leaning but explicitly flagged Low reliability (31% odds higher in 4 weeks, typical -3.1% move, 50pp spread), while weather (priority #2) is strongly bullish - a genuine conflict at the top of the priority stack. Storage (+184 Bcf above 5y, +72 Bcf bearish build surprise) and a 50<200 EMA cross on the daily/weekly chart lean bearish, but managed money is only moderately net short (35th percentile), leaving no side of this with a clean, well-supported edge for a multi-week commitment.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) price to push toward and hold above ~$3.10-3.15 (near the +14D/+21D fair-value path) alongside continued above-normal weather demand, confirming a bullish breakout toward the $3.35-3.45 fair-value band top, or (b) a drop into the $2.60-2.70 zone (near the fair-value band's lower quartile) alongside weather demand fading back toward the 5y norm and another bearish storage surprise, confirming a seasonal mean-reversion short.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7 day and 8-16 day weather demand are 15-24% above the 5y normal near the top of their historical ranges and still rising, the futures curve is in backwardation from front to Sep (-$0.048) consistent with near-term tightness, and daily/4h RSI are oversold (26-36, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) just as 5m/15m momentum (RSI 59-66, positive MACD) is already turning up - a classic short-term bounce setup into peak summer cooling demand.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back below ~$2.76, or a shift in the 1-7D/8-16D weather forecast back toward or below the 5y normal, would remove the basis for the bounce.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand 15.4% (1-7D) and 23.9% (8-16D) above 5y normal, near top of historical range and still rising
Price essentially at fair value now ($2.948 vs $2.917 fair) with the fair-value path rising to $3.067 by +21D
Daily/4h oversold technicals (RSI 26-36, below lower Bollinger) vs turning-up intraday momentum - bounce setup
Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a 72 Bcf bearish build surprise caps the medium-term upside
Seasonality is bearish-leaning but explicitly Low reliability (31-38% odds higher over 1-4 weeks); managed money only moderately net short (35th percentile)
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly Low (50pp spread) - the historical pattern could fail in either direction
